assembly_instructions: Read Gemma 3 as a DENSE multimodal stack. Tokens (and image patches!) enter at the floor (X=0,Y=0). Image inputs go through the SigLIP-400M vision encoder on the left at X=-5 — the orange Pan&Scan slicer fans non-square images into multiple 896×896 crops, each crop becomes 256 'soft tokens'. Text and image tokens are concatenated and climb the green residual pillar through 62 dense Transformer blocks. The killer Gemma-3 trick is the LOCAL : GLOBAL attention pattern, 5 LOCAL (sliding-window=1024, RoPE base 10K) blocks for every 1 GLOBAL block (full-context, RoPE base 1M). Local blocks are coloured pale-mint, global blocks are deep-emerald — count them on the side. Each block has dual RMSNorm (pre + post) on both attention and FFN, GQA (32 Q heads sharing 16 KV heads), QK-Norm in place of soft-cap, and a dense SwiGLU FFN (no MoE here — Gemma 3 is fully dense). The amber column on the left at X=-3 is the GQA KV cache, dramatically smaller because 5/6 of layers only ever cache a 1024-token window. The translucent slab at X=8 marks the parallelism plan + distillation-from-larger-teacher. Click any part for FLOPs, memory, parallelism class, scratch-build pseudocode, the dominant bottleneck, and improvement ideas.

info.summary: 27B-parameter DENSE decoder-only multimodal LM from Google DeepMind. 62 layers × 5376 hidden dim, 32 query heads / 16 KV heads (GQA, ratio 2), SwiGLU FFN, dual RMSNorm (pre + post) on every sublayer, QK-Norm, and the signature 5:1 LOCAL-to-GLOBAL attention pattern (sliding-window 1024 + full-context). Vision through a frozen SigLIP-400M encoder with Pan-and-Scan, 896×896 crops, 256 soft-tokens per image. 128K context (32K for the 1B variant), trained on ~14T tokens with knowledge distillation from a larger teacher.
info.description:
Gemma 3 is the third generation of Google's open Gemma family. Compared with Gemma 2 it (i) goes multimodal via SigLIP + Pan&Scan, (ii) extends context 8K → 128K, (iii) replaces attention soft-cap with QK-Norm, (iv) uses an extreme 5:1 ratio of LOCAL (sliding-window) to GLOBAL attention layers — local layers do almost all the work, global layers carry long-range information. Local layers use RoPE base 10,000 (standard); global layers use RoPE base 1,000,000 to keep extrapolation clean to 128K. Both pre-norm AND post-norm RMSNorm are applied to attention and FFN — empirically improves training stability of fp16/bf16 deep stacks. The model is DENSE (no MoE) — every parameter activates for every token. Trained on ~14 T tokens via large-batch SGD with knowledge distillation from a larger Gemini teacher.

Design rationale, in one line each:
• 5:1 local:global: makes long-context KV cheap because 5/6 of layers only need a 1024-token window.
• QK-Norm: removes attention-logit blow-ups at long context without the soft-cap's training instability.
• Dual norm (pre+post): cheap stability win for very deep stacks.
• SigLIP + Pan&Scan: handles non-square / high-resolution images without resize artefacts.
• Distillation from teacher: smaller models inherit teacher knowledge cheaply.

facts.label: QK norm
facts.value: RMSNorm on Q and K (replaces soft-cap)

facts.label: norm placement
facts.value: Pre-norm AND post-norm RMSNorm on each sublayer

facts.label: image input
facts.value: Pan-and-Scan: non-square images sliced into multiple crops

facts.label: distillation
facts.value: Knowledge distillation from a larger Gemini teacher

comparisons.delta: Gemma 2 was text-only with 8K context, alternating local-global attention at a milder ratio (1:1), used attention soft-cap (logit clipping) for stability, and pre-norm only. Gemma 3 keeps the dense architecture but adds vision (SigLIP + Pan&Scan), extends context to 128K, swaps soft-cap for QK-Norm, pushes the local:global ratio to 5:1 (massive KV win), and adds post-norm RMSNorm. Vocab 256K stays.

Gemma 1 was vanilla text-only LLaMA-style: pre-norm RMSNorm, RoPE, SwiGLU, MHA. Gemma 3 keeps the surface but replaces literally every attention block with a 5:1 local-global GQA + QK-Norm + dual-norm hybrid, adds multimodal input via SigLIP, jumps from 8K to 128K context, uses distillation from a larger teacher, and is much wider/deeper at the 27B point.

LLaMA-3 70B and Mistral-Large are GQA dense decoders with full-context attention and standard pre-norm. Gemma 3 trades width/depth for a smarter attention plan — 5/6 of its layers are local (1024-token window) so its KV cache is 5–10× smaller at 128K context for similar quality. It is also natively multimodal where most peers bolt vision on as a separate model.

Gemma 3 is fundamentally different: DENSE (every parameter active for every token) where V3/GLM are sparse MoE. Trade-off: at 27B Gemma 3 activates 27B per token (vs ~32B for GLM-4.5 at 355B total), so Gemma 3 hits more compute per token relative to capacity. Gemma 3 has no expert all-to-all bottleneck — single-node inference is dramatically simpler. It's also smaller in absolute capacity but matches MoE peers up to its size class because of the strong attention plan and distillation.

parts.name: Input Tokens (text, V=256K)
parts.role: Text input via the multilingual SentencePiece tokeniser (V=256K). Image tokens get prepended after the vision pipeline.

parts.name: Input Image
parts.role: Raw image input. Variable resolution and aspect ratio.
parts.algorithm: image: H x W x 3 RGB

parts.name: Pan-and-Scan Slicer
parts.role: Algorithm that splits non-square / high-resolution images into multiple 896×896 crops with overlap. Avoids the aspect-ratio distortion of naive resize.
improvement_ideas: Adaptive crop count based on image content (text-heavy vs natural).

parts.name: SigLIP-400M (frozen vision encoder)
parts.role: Frozen Sigmoid-Loss-Image-Pretraining ViT, 400M params, 14×14 patches → 64×64=4096 → MAP-pooled and projected to 256 'soft tokens' per crop.
parts.algorithm:
patches = patchify(crop, 14)
features = vit(patches)               # 4096 patches
soft_tokens = average_pool(features)  # → 256 tokens
improvement_ideas: Cache soft tokens for repeated images.
Replace MAP pooling with Q-Former for richer summarisation.

parts.name: Vision-Text Projector
parts.role: Linear projector mapping SigLIP features into the LLM's d=5376 embedding space. 256 soft tokens per crop are inserted into the text stream at <image> placeholders.
parts.algorithm:
soft_h = soft_tokens @ W_proj   # → [256, 5376]
# inserted at <image> position in the text sequence

parts.name: Embedding (V=256K, d=5376)
parts.role: Text token embedding lookup, tied with the LM head. 1.38 B params (~2.75 GiB BF16).

parts.name: Residual Stream (d=5376, 62 layers)
parts.role: Pre-norm + post-norm residual carry. y = x + post_norm(sublayer(pre_norm(x))).
parts.algorithm:
x = x + post_attn(attn(pre_attn(x)))
x = x + post_ffn(ffn(pre_ffn(x)))

parts.name: × 62 Layers (5 LOCAL : 1 GLOBAL pattern)
parts.role: Marker for the 62-layer depth. Pattern repeats: L L L L L G L L L L L G ... — 5 local layers (sliding-window 1024) per 1 global layer (full context).

parts.name: Block 1 — LOCAL (window=1024, RoPE 10K)
parts.role: Local sliding-window attention block. Each token attends only to the previous 1024 tokens. RoPE base 10,000.

parts.name: Block 2 — LOCAL
parts.role: Local block 2.

parts.name: Block 3 — LOCAL
parts.role: Local block 3.

parts.name: Block 4 — LOCAL
parts.role: Local block 4.

parts.name: Block 5 — LOCAL
parts.role: Local block 5.

parts.name: Block 6 — GLOBAL (full ctx, RoPE 1M)
parts.role: GLOBAL attention block — attends to the full context, uses RoPE base 1,000,000 to keep angles distinct out to 128K. ~6× bigger compute share at long ctx.

parts.name: Block 7 — LOCAL
parts.role: Local block 7. Pattern resumes.

parts.name: Block 12 — GLOBAL (later in stack)
parts.role: Another GLOBAL block. (Layers 8–11 elided.)

parts.name: Block 60 — LOCAL
parts.role: Late-stack local block. (Layers 13–59 elided.)

parts.name: Block 62 — GLOBAL (final)
parts.role: Final block before the LM head — global, gives the head full-context summary.

parts.name: Pre-Attn RMSNorm
parts.role: Pre-norm RMSNorm before attention. γ ∈ R^d learned.

parts.name: GQA · QKV Proj (32 Q / 16 KV)
parts.role: Fused QKV projection. 32 query heads share 16 KV heads (group ratio 2). Each head is 128-dim.
parts.algorithm:
Q = x @ W_Q  # [B,T, 32, 128]
K = x @ W_K  # [B,T, 16, 128]
V = x @ W_V  # [B,T, 16, 128]
Q, K = qk_rmsnorm(Q, K)
Q, K = apply_rope(Q, K, base=10K_local OR 1M_global)

parts.name: QK-Norm (replaces soft-cap)
parts.role: RMSNorm on Q and K BEFORE the dot product. Cleaner long-context behaviour than Gemma-2's logit soft-cap.

parts.name: RoPE (10K local / 1M global)
parts.role: Two RoPE bases live in the model: 10K for local layers (standard), 1M for global layers (extends cleanly to 128K).
parts.algorithm:
freqs = base ** (-2*i/d)
Q_rot = (Q[..., even] * cos) - (Q[..., odd] * sin)

parts.name: FlashAttention-2 (local OR global)
parts.role: Same kernel, different mask. LOCAL layers apply a sliding-window mask (last 1024 tokens). GLOBAL layers apply a standard causal mask over the full context.
parts.flops_per_token: Local: ≈ 2·n_h·d_h·1024 ≈ 8 MFLOP. Global: ≈ 2·n_h·d_h·T ≈ 1 GFLOP at T=128K.
improvement_ideas: Ring-attention sharding for global layers at >256K context.
Sparse global attention (e.g. BigBird) if context grows further.

parts.name: Attention · O-Projection
parts.role: Output projection back to d=5376.

parts.name: Post-Attn RMSNorm
parts.role: POST-norm RMSNorm before adding back into the residual. Gemma-3 is one of the few models that does pre+post RMSNorm — small but consistent stability win.

parts.name: GQA KV Cache (5/6 layers windowed)
parts.role: Hetero KV cache. LOCAL layers (5/6 of total) only need to retain the last 1024 tokens — those layers' KV is essentially free at long context. GLOBAL layers (1/6) hold the full context but at a 16-head GQA rate.
parts.memory_footprint: Local: 1024 × 16 × 128 × 2 × FP16 × ~52 layers ≈ 200 MB. Global: 128K × 16 × 128 × 2 × FP16 × ~10 layers ≈ 5 GB. Total much smaller than full-MHA.
improvement_ideas: INT4 KV quantisation on global layers (where the bulk lives at 128K).

parts.name: FFN · Pre-Norm RMSNorm
parts.role: Pre-norm before the SwiGLU FFN.

parts.name: SwiGLU FFN (d_ff = 21504, dense)
parts.role: Dense SwiGLU FFN. NO MoE — every parameter activates for every token. d_ff = 21504 ≈ 4×d.
parts.flops_per_token: ≈ 3 × 2·d·d_ff ≈ 690 MFLOP / layer (largest single GEMM in the block)
parts.memory_footprint: 3 × d × d_ff × BF16 ≈ 692 MB / layer; 62 layers ≈ 43 GB total FFN params
parts.algorithm:
h = silu(x @ W_gate) * (x @ W_up)
y = h @ W_down
residual += post_norm(y)
parts.bottleneck: Largest GEMM in the model — soaks tensor cores at training, becomes bandwidth-bound at decode (small batch).
improvement_ideas: FP8 / FP4 weight quantisation for inference.
Sequence-parallel FFN to overlap with attention of the next layer.

parts.name: FFN · Post-Norm RMSNorm
parts.role: Post-norm RMSNorm before adding the FFN output back to the residual.

parts.name: LM Head (tied, V=256K)
parts.role: Tied projection back to the 256K vocab.
parts.algorithm:
logits = h @ E.T
p      = softmax(logits / T)

parts.name: LM Loss + Distillation Loss
parts.role: Cross-entropy on next-token PLUS KL-distillation against a larger teacher's softmax.

parts.name: Teacher (larger Gemini)
parts.role: Frozen larger Gemini model whose softmax outputs the student distils against. Crucial for matching MoE peers at 27B.

parts.name: AdamW + large batch
parts.role: AdamW with large batch (millions of tokens / step) on TPU pods. β1=0.9, β2=0.95.

parts.name: TPU-pod Parallelism (TP × DP × FSDP)
parts.role: Trained on TPU pods rather than GPUs. Plan: TP within pod-slice, FSDP for parameter/optimiser sharding, DP across pod-slices. No EP because the model is dense.
improvement_ideas: Sequence-parallel norms to remove TP-replicated activations.

parts.name: 14 T-token Multilingual Corpus
parts.role: Pre-training data plate: ~14T tokens of multilingual text + interleaved image-text pairs for the vision-language phase.
